senses.gloss: clothing, anything that is worn on the body (tops, trousers, dresses, armours, etc.)

etymology.text: Iranian in origin, but the details are not understood; it is pre-Islamic, probably from Aramaic סַרְבְּלָא (sarbəlā), סַרְבַּלָא (sarbalā, “mantle; trousers”) or Aramaic סַרְבֵּל (sarbel, “to cloth with a mantle; to clothe with trousers”), and it is possible that the verbs came before the noun in Arabic and the noun is deverbal. Regard Persian شلوار (šalvâr, “trousers”).

body.text: (S, M, Msb, K:) and [so in the M, and in the Ham p. 65, but in the Msb and K “ or ”]
(M, Msb, K:) the former is the primary signification: (Ham p. 349:) or
, (Msb, TA,) which occurs in the Kur xvi. 83 [in the first and also in the second of the senses mentioned above]: (TA:) and
being, accord. to Yaakoob, a substitute for the
. (M.) ―         -b2-  In the following words of a trad. of 'Othmán,
I will not pull off a garment with which God has invested me
